key_points: Dorm assignment doesn't define your experience — upperclassmen confirm this
Unauthorized guests living in suites is a real enforcement problem
Room inspections exist but RAs can't open belongings
key_quotes: The dorm you're in will not make or break your experience
I live in a single room with a bathroom and a few weeks after last semester ended, I noticed some guy had moved in with my suitemate
She said she can't cancel the plan... it's been less than a week

key_points: Greek life dominates — hard to find tight friend groups outside it
Most students don't lock in their real friend group until sophomore year
Being sober or non-drinking is possible but you're definitely the exception
key_quotes: As a woman who was friends with guys rushing freshman year I don't think I ever met one who was sober
It's a huge drug campus
I didn't find my people until sophomore year

key_points: Free food at campus events is a consistent money-saving tip
Bring Tupperware to the dining hall — students smuggle food out openly
No major complaints but no praise either — largely ignored topic
key_quotes: Bring tupperware to the dining hall. Be discreet about it, but back in undergrad I brought so much salad, cooked chicken back to my apartment
Many events have free food. Support the event, meet new people, and get free food. Win, win, win
Don't buy any spirit wear t-shirts, you will be given plenty for free your freshman year

key_points: Introverts and non-social students report feeling isolated and misfit
A freshman broke down crying within days of moving in, felt totally unprepared
Finding community takes time — students warn against expecting instant belonging
key_quotes: I moved into my dorm a little less than 2 days ago and I instantly started breaking down and crying
I'm a junior. I still only have a couple of friends and no friend group
If you aren't a social person then Tulane might not be for you

key_points: Financial aid office has widespread reputation for being rude and unhelpful
Students report being told to sell their house to qualify for more aid
Aid packages for spring admits and transfer students are often worse or missing
key_quotes: Tulane's financial aid department are total assholes and They Do Not Value You
They basically told me once that I didn't qualify for more because of the value of my family's house so we should sell the house
I didn't get my aid for last year until the first week of November

key_points: STEM and pre-med classes have serious quality issues — bad professors, steep curves
Physics, math, and chemistry departments specifically called out as weak
School caters heavily to pre-med, business, and law — other majors feel underserved
key_quotes: My first semester general chemistry class was probably the worse experience ever and my professor could of cared less about actually teaching
I transferred from Tulane after freshman year. The institution caters mostly to premed, business, and law
Pre med sucks, bio weed is real, and all bio and most of chem prof for the pre med class suck

key_points: Multiple students report administration doesn't genuinely care about students
Demonstrated interest matters heavily in admissions — more than at most schools
Admissions process frustrates international students who face near-impossible odds for aid
key_quotes: I've only been here 3 semesters and can count like 4 or 5 instances where they demonstrated they don't care
There has been so much funding cut, all these schools want to know you can pay
Don't be too hung up on this rejection; it's well-known how much wealthy kids Tulane accepts

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — consistent praise across Reddit and YouTube
New Orleans crime is real; at least one student was robbed at gunpoint one block from campus
Hurricane season overlaps with fall semester — a practical risk students underestimate
key_quotes: My senior year I was robbed at gunpoint at the corner of Willow and Calhoun — literally a block off campus
The fall semester starts at the peak of hurricane season. Over 4 years you're likely to at least once have your semester disrupted
New Orleans is real AF. You step off campus and you encounter a city that's split in two socioeconomically

key_points: Alumni network described as limited outside pre-med, business, and law pipelines
Research opportunities exist but are narrow in scope
Energy industry alumni note poor campus event promotion and low student engagement
key_quotes: I feel like it's pretty easy to get into research, but it's pretty limited in terms of what's offered
I'm an alumni in the energy industry who heard about it from my fiancé in an unrelated masters program at Tulane. They definitely could have gotten the word out
The institution caters mostly to premed, business, and law. It's definitely not just you

key_points: Tuition approaching $100k/year with financial aid office that actively discourages need-based applicants
Merit scholarships exist but are inconsistent — spring admits often get nothing
Louisiana residents have special scholarship programs that make attendance genuinely affordable
key_quotes: I can't afford 90k+ a year, but also do not qualify for fasfa or need based aid
Even if I get in, how will I afford it
Tulane has special scholarship programs for both Louisiana and New Orleans residents

key_points: Wealthy, white, Northeast-heavy student body is the dominant social culture
New Orleans itself is the real selling point — students who engage with the city love it
Bubble is real but optional — you have to actively choose to leave it
key_quotes: Everyone is rich and white and the worst person you've ever met
Diversity here is getting better, but it's still pretty bad. I'm from Louisiana, and Tulane couldn't care less about LA
The bubble is less pronounced at Tulane than most other urban campuses. New Orleans is real AF

key_points: Safety: student robbed at gunpoint one block from campus; crime is not just rumor
Financial Aid: office described as actively hostile to students seeking need-based help
Diversity: Black and minority students struggle to find community; sorority racism scandal documented
key_quotes: My senior year I was robbed at gunpoint at the corner of Willow and Calhoun — literally a block off campus. Yes, it is dangerous
Tulane's financial aid department are total assholes and They Do Not Value You
I have a very middle eastern name and when I attended Tulane in 2023 I literally could not find ANY accommodation until 2 weeks before I arrived

key_points: Tulane Pathway to Medicine is a near-guaranteed med school route for top applicants
NSF-REU summer research program pays $7k stipend plus free housing — open to any US undergrad
Community Service Fellowship and Weatherhead scholarships are low-profile but meaningful awards
key_quotes: Less than 30 people get invited per year. If you get in, you have a guaranteed spot
Tulane has a new NSF-REU funded summer research program open to undergrads from any US university. $7,000 stipend, free housing, travel covered
I got deferred in 2017 and got in thru RD! Keep showing interest
